hints.text: Plates **black with 2 white sections** (Indonesia = 3 sections); drives on the **left**
**Double white centre lines** (only SE Asian country to do so); thick **yellow bar** markings ~exclusive to Malaysia
Electricity poles often have **black or white stickers** (sometimes red-white / red-blue stripes below)
**BERHENTI** (stop) & **BERI LALUAN** (yield) signs are unique to Malaysia; bollards in black/white/grey/red
Directional signs w/ **hexagonal yellow highway shields** (unique); km markers white-and-blue
**Malay** (Latin, ≈ Indonesian); **Chinese characters** common (≈25% of Malaysians are of Chinese descent)
Signposts almost always **black-and-white striped**; black-and-white kerbs common
Each state has its **own road-letter prefix**; state flags appear regularly (worth learning)
**Postal codes on most street signs** → narrow the region
Pole stickers: black = mainland, **white = Sarawak (Borneo)**; 'TEL TNB' stickers = Johor
Borneo: **Sabah very mountainous** (much coverage on mountain roads); Sarawak flatter, wooden diagonal-support poles; isolated karst near Perlis (Thai border)
Arrow-shaped street signs (Malaysia-unique): **yellow = Johor**, green w/ JKR hexagon = Sabah; trash-bin logos cluster by state
